videoJson.name: VHS
videoJson.fact1: A smaller variant  of VHS, VHS-C,  was introduced  in 1982 for use  in camcorders;  these tapes  could be played  in standard VHS  machines with  an adaptor
videoJson.fact2: Development  of this format  was initially  cancelled in  1972, but  engineers  continued  to work on  it in secret  until they had  a successful  working  prototype  in 1973
videoJson.fact3: This format was  developed with the  goals of being  compatible with  any television  and across producing  companies, ability to  be customized and  expanded, inexpensive  to purchase and repair,  and have parts that can  be easily replaced  and maintained
